Avatar: The Way of Water (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $688,809,501Details
International Box Office $1,634,092,522
Worldwide Box Office $2,322,902,023
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,794,041
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $15,228,241
Total $25,022,282
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Set more than a decade after the events of the first film, “Avatar: The Way of Water” begins to tell the story of the Sully family (Jake, Neytiri, and their kids), the trouble that follows them, the lengths they go to keep each other safe, the battles they fight to stay alive, and the tragedies they endure.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$134,100,226 (19.5% of total gross)
Legs:5.14 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$400,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,202 opening theaters/4,340 max. theaters, 11.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $769,309,430

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 16th, 2022 (Wide) by 20th Century Studios
December 16th, 2022 (IMAX) by 20th Century Studios
October 3rd, 2025 (IMAX) by 20th Century Studios
October 3rd, 2025 (Wide) by 20th Century Studios
International Releases: December 9th, 2022 (Wide) (Finland)
December 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Germany)
December 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Switzerland)
December 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Philippines)
December 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Belgium)
Video Release: March 28th, 2023 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of strong violence and intense action, partial nudity and some strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2759 (cert #54081), 11/23/2022)
Running Time: 190 minutes
Franchise: Avatar
Award nomination: 2023 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Legacy Sequel, Sequel With Returning Stars Playing New Characters
Filming location (city or town): Los Angeles, CA
Plot point: Humans as Aliens
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Lightstorm Entertainment, 20th Century Studios, TSG Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Set more than a decade after the events of the first film, “Avatar: The Way of Water” begins to tell the story of the Sully family (Jake, Neytiri, and their kids), the trouble that follows them, the lengths they go to keep each other safe, the battles they fight to stay alive, and the tragedies they endure.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$134,100,226 (19.5% of total gross)
Legs:5.14 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$400,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,202 opening theaters/4,340 max. theaters, 11.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $769,309,430

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 16th, 2022 (Wide) by 20th Century Studios
December 16th, 2022 (IMAX) by 20th Century Studios
October 3rd, 2025 (IMAX) by 20th Century Studios
October 3rd, 2025 (Wide) by 20th Century Studios
International Releases: December 9th, 2022 (Wide) (Finland)
December 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Germany)
December 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Switzerland)
December 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Philippines)
December 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Belgium)
Video Release: March 28th, 2023 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of strong violence and intense action, partial nudity and some strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2759 (cert #54081), 11/23/2022)
Running Time: 190 minutes
Franchise: Avatar
Award nomination: 2023 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Legacy Sequel, Sequel With Returning Stars Playing New Characters
Filming location (city or town): Los Angeles, CA
Plot point: Humans as Aliens
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Lightstorm Entertainment, 20th Century Studios, TSG Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
